task: Make FrankX Music learning the canonical free authority layer with proof-safe claims and premium usable navigation.
audience: Independent adult learners, creator-musicians, and family-supported younger learners
first_read: Free self-paced music learning that leads to one observable artifact, not a second paid academy.

references.label: FrankX design and taste contracts
references.reason: Brand restraint, obsidian material, technical evidence, and no AI-slop language

references.label: NIH/NCCIH Music and Health
references.reason: Research uncertainty and distinction between listening content and credentialed music therapy

avoid: Child-app hero as the canonical front door
Decorative motion and generic card walls
direction.signature: Understand, practice, make: every route names one observable learning artifact.
system: Obsidian near-solid surfaces with restrained emerald and cyan signals
Static hierarchy by default with visible keyboard focus
Exact privacy, guardian, rights, and evidence boundaries

asset_quality.why_premium: The primary visual is the real curriculum and route system, authored as exact UI rather than decorative media or fake product proof.

artifacts.notes: Inspected at 1440x900, 390x844, 640px reflow, and reduced motion; no overflow, clipping, or running animation.
Inspected at 1440x900 and 390x844 with claim-boundary state; no overflow, clipping, or page error.

checks.name: asset-tier
checks.evidence: Tier C exact curriculum UI is the real product proof; no external or generated hero asset is used.

checks.name: export-inspection
checks.evidence: Twelve local production screenshots inspected for hub and science first viewport and primary state.

checks.name: visual-qa
checks.evidence: 1440x900, 390x844, 640px reflow, reduced motion, keyboard focus, primary navigation, and page-error checks pass. Local-only global auth and Vercel telemetry requests are recorded separately.

score.notes: 5/5 first read; 5/5 brand fit; 5/5 craft; 5/5 accessibility/responsiveness/reduced motion; 4/5 factual/provenance due external-source freshness dependency; 4/5 utility because legacy subroutes retain mixed visual systems.

known_risks: Legacy subroutes retain mixed light/dark visual systems and decorative motion.
External teacher, tool, and score links can change independently of FrankX.
Piano and violin remain German-first rather than fully bilingual.
